Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal For Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar Jan-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Carlos Montelongo, 415-410-4613 Plaintiff in Pro Per. (Party leaves before the Court takes the bench)
No other Appearance.

The clerk informs Mr. Montelongo that the Court did not receive any contest emails, therefore the matter will not be called. The matter is not called. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MANZAR AZARI, AZARI PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, THE AZARI GROUP REAL ESTATE, INC., AZARI REAL ESTATE BROKERAGE, JOHN EVANGELATOS, LUZON CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT, ERICK CATALAN'S Motion For Dismissal For Failure To Prosecute.

Defendants Azari Property Management, Azari Real Estate Brokerage, Manzar Azari, Erick Catalan, John Evangalatos, Luzon Construction Management and The Azari Group Real Estate, Inc.'s motion to dismiss action for failure to prosecute, which is unopposed, is granted. The action is subject to mandatory dismissal because Plaintiff did not bring it to trial within five years of its commencement. (See Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 583.310.) Additionally, the court dismisses the action because Plaintiff has not shown reasonable diligence in prosecution and the action has been pending for (well over) two years. (See Code Civ. Proc., Sections 583.410 & 583.420.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling approved as to form.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Off Calendar Sep-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Carlos Montelongo (415-756-8341) appears as plaintiff, In Propria Persona

Attorney, Alex Tovarian of Ongaro PC (415-984-9990) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANTS MANZAR AZARI, AZARI PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, THE AZARI GROUP REAL ESTATE, INC., AZARI REAL ESTATE BROKERAGE, LUZON CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT, ERICK CATALAN AND JOHN EVANGALATOS' Motion To Compel Arbitration And Request For Stay Of Proceedings is GRANTED. If there is any arbitration needed, it shall take place in Marin County. Per the Federal Arbitration Act and the California Arbitration Act, the law favors arbitration and a waiver will not be lightly inferred. The party claiming a waiver "bears a heavy burden of proof." (Saint Agnes Med. Ctr. v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1195.) Plaintiff fails to meet that burden. Although defendants delayed seeking arbitration and plaintiff was compelled to respond to defendants' demurrers, that conduct is insufficient to find a waiver because plaintiff has not shown that his ability to participate in the arbitration has been impaired. (See Groom v. Health Net (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1189, 1196-1197.) The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - CARLOS MONTELONGO, TEL. NO. 1-415-756-8341, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. DAVIN R. BACHO OF THE BLEDSOE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-981-5411 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MANZAR AZARI, AZARI PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, THE AZARI GROUP REAL ESTATE INC, AZARI REAL ESTATE BROKERAGE, LUZON CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT, ERICK CATALAN and JOHN EVANGELATOS' DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained, with twenty days leave to amend. As currently pled, Plaintiff's second amended complaint is uncertain and does not plead facts which support the causes of action. (CCP section 430.10(f) and (e).) The basis of Plaintiff's first cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law is unclear and ambiguous. Plaintiff is provided leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege clear and concise facts which support the cause of action. To the extent the claim is based on fraudulent acts, the allegations must be specifically pled. Plaintiff appears to allege facts which could support a cause of action for breach of contract. If he can do so in good faith, Plaintiff is provided leave to amend the second cause of action to plead facts which identify the contract(s) at issue and which show that he performed under the contract(s), Defendants breached the contract(s), and that he was damaged. The allegation that Defendants refused to answer Plaintiff's calls and the conclusion that he suffered emotional distress do not support Plaintiff's claim of intentional infliction of emotional distress. If he can do so in good faith, Plaintiff is provided an opportunity to amend the third cause of action to plead facts showing that Defendants engaged in extreme and outrageous conduct and that he suffered severe emotional distress. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter; Rose Miller, CSR #5041

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - CARLOS MONTELONGO, TEL. NO. 1-415-756-8341, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. DAVIN R. BACHO OF THE BLEDSOE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-981-5411 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MANZAR AZARI, AZARI PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, THE AZARI GROUP REAL ESTATE, INC., AZARI REAL ESTATE BROKERAGE, JOHN EVANGELATOS, LUZON CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT, AND ERICK CATALAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendants' demurrer to all three claims is sustained with thirty days leave to amend. While it appears that plaintiff seeks to allege claims for breach of contract, violation of the Unfair Competition Law and some tort allowing recovery for emotional distress, the complaint is vague, difficult to follow, at times unintelligible and does not comply with the basic rule requiring a statement of facts in ordinary and concise language that supports the legal theories on which plaintiff seeks to recover damages. Because it appears reasonably possible that plaintiff can state one or more claims against one or more defendants, plaintiff is given leave to do so. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/hk
